error_signature: HTTP request does not return any value
title: Handling missing Jira ID in HTTP request
symptom: When the HTTP request to find a Jira ID fails, no error is thrown, and the workflow does not create a Jira ticket.
applies_when: Using HTTP request to fetch Jira ID
Email does not belong to an existing Jira user
not_this_if: Using a different method to handle missing IDs
root_cause: The HTTP request node in n8n does not output any data when the requested Jira ID is not found, leading to silent failures in the workflow. This can cause subsequent nodes to not execute as expected.
fix_steps: Enable the 'Always output data' option in the HTTP request settings.
Modify the code to check if the response contains data and return a default account ID if not.
agent_instruction: Enable 'Always output data' in the HTTP request settings and update the code to handle missing Jira IDs.
